page: Amazon Web Services r6i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all ranking in the top 25%. Single-core performance for stress-ng and Geekbench falls in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth tests show strong results in the top 25% for both cached small block and uncached large block reads and writes. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, with the PassMark CPU encryption test placing in the top 10%, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES, VAES, and SHA_NI.

The instance exhibits a clear tradeoff between multi-threaded scaling and single-threaded execution. While multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with Clang score in the top 25%, single-threaded gzip compression and text processing remain in the middle 50%. Large-scale LLM inference shows a significant bottleneck, with text generation for a 70B model falling into the bottom 25%. This resource profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving, where its strong multi-core scaling and high memory capacity can be fully leveraged.
description: Amazon Web Services r6i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 38 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores in the top 25%, alongside top-tier cryptographic performance in the top 10%. However, single-core execution and large-scale LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the middle and bottom quartiles respectively. This resource profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 38 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6i.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance delivering high-density DDR4 RAM and strong multi-core processing for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz with dedicated CPU allocation.
Strong multi-core and cryptographic performance, placing in the top 25% and top 10% respectively.
38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators.
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and multi-threaded software compilation.
